title: Seismic Control FAIL: quiet → surface_wave in 1 illegal direct jump (ILLEGAL_TRANSITION)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates ILLEGAL_TRANSITION: the generator sequence claims quiet→surface_wave in a single move 'apply_lowpass', but the control_graph has no edge (quiet, apply_lowpass, surface_wave). The only legal edge from quiet is (quiet, increase_gain, p_wave). Because the claimed transition is not in the control graph, the cert fails with ILLEGAL_TRANSITION. Additionally, since the path never reaches the target via any legal route, GOAL_NOT_REACHED is also raised.

setup.filter_chain: gain→bandpass→lowpass chain matches QA generator alphabet
setup.notes: Illegal direct jump attempt: quiet→surface_wave with a single lowpass filter. No such edge exists in the seismic control graph. The quiet state can only be escaped via increase_gain, which transitions to p_wave. This is the canonical illegal-transition certificate for QA_SEISMIC_CONTROL_CERT.v1.

generator_correspondence.qa_generator_role: ILLEGAL: no generator maps singularity directly to cosmos; singularity requires threshold-crossing expansion first

validation_checks.description: path_length_k equals number of generator steps
final pattern class reaches target pattern class
all transitions are legal edges in control_graph
validation_checks.details: quiet-apply_lowpass->surface_wave: ILLEGAL — no such edge in control_graph; only legal edge from quiet is (quiet, increase_gain, p_wave)

validation_checks.description: final pattern class maps to recognized QA orbit family
final orbit family is not out_of_orbit (not disordered)

fail_ledger.message: Step 1: move='apply_lowpass' from state 'quiet' has no legal edge in control_graph. Legal moves from quiet: [increase_gain→p_wave]. The transition quiet→surface_wave via apply_lowpass does not exist; it would skip the mandatory threshold-crossing expansion from singularity to satellite orbit.
Final state 'quiet' does not match target_pattern_class 'surface_wave'. The illegal move failed to advance the state; the system remained in the initial quiet/singularity configuration.
